name: Protocol Step
description: A lab-protocol step card rendering a numbered procedural step with action description, reagent list with volumes and concentrations, required equipment chips, timing controls, safety indicators, and completion confirmation checkbox.

standard.description: Detailed protocol step showing action, full reagent list with volumes, equipment chips, safety badges, and completion checkbox. Intended as primary rendering during active bench work with all context visible.

checklist.description: Compact single-line rendering showing step number, abbreviated action, and completion checkbox. Reagents collapse into a popover. Used for quick-scan protocol overviews and printable bench cards.

timed.description: Step optimized for time-critical procedures featuring a prominent countdown/count-up TimerDisplay with start/pause/reset controls. Completion auto-suggests when timer completes with audible chime.

visual_brief: A lab-notebook-inspired step card rendered as a d-surface with a thin left border in primary accent color. At the top-left corner, a StepNumber renders as a 40px circular badge with the number in bold text ('3') against a filled primary-color background. To its right, the StepAction contains the procedural instruction in text-base semibold followed by an optional description in body-muted text (e.g., 'Add lysis buffer and vortex for 30 seconds'). Below the header, the ReagentList renders as a bordered sub-panel with a d-annotation caption 'Reagents'. Each ReagentRow contains: reagent name (semibold), volume (mono-data, e.g., '50 µL'), concentration (mono-data muted, e.g., '10 mM'), and a small source-chip indicating which stock or lot (e.g., 'Lot #A2394'). Adjacent to ReagentList, EquipmentChips render as pill-shaped tags with tiny iconography (vortex mixer, centrifuge, pipette, heat block, incubator) showing the instruments required. SafetyBadges render as a horizontal row of color-coded pills with warning icons: amber for PPE requirements (gloves, eye protection), red for hazardous materials (flammable, corrosive, toxic), blue for specialized handling (fume hood, biosafety cabinet). For timed steps, the TimerDisplay sits prominently as a large digital-style countdown card (text-3xl mono-data) with start/pause/reset icon buttons. The timer background shifts from neutral to amber when <30s remain and pulses when time expires. A NotesBox sits below as a small textarea for scientist observations. At the bottom-right, the CompletionCheck is a large 24px checkbox labeled 'Mark complete' that, when checked, locks the card in a completed state (subtle green tint, strikethrough action text) and records a timestamp chip showing who completed it and when. Completed cards show a thin success-colored left border replacing the primary border.

layout_hints.container: d-surface card with colored left border
layout_hints.note: Protocol steps are safety-critical scientific procedures. Every reagent MUST show volume AND concentration. Every hazard MUST have a visible SafetyBadge. Completion MUST be timestamped.
layout_hints.step_number: StepNumber uses d-annotation[data-step-number] as a 40px filled circle with bold number. Completed steps show checkmark instead of number. Primary color filled.
layout_hints.reagent_format: ReagentRow uses mono-data tabular-nums for all volumes and concentrations. Format: 'Reagent Name | 50 µL | 10 mM | Lot #A2394'. Never abbreviate units.
layout_hints.safety_badges: SafetyBadge colors MUST map consistently: amber=PPE, red=hazard, blue=specialized-handling. Each badge includes icon+label, never icon-only (unparseable without label).
layout_hints.timer_states: TimerDisplay transitions through states: idle (neutral), running (primary), warning (<30s amber), expired (red pulse). Always shows mm:ss format with tabular-nums.
layout_hints.completion_lock: CompletionCheck is single-direction (checking locks the step). Undoing completion requires supervisor password to maintain protocol integrity and audit trail.

slots.step-header: Top row containing the circular StepNumber badge on the left and the StepAction instruction text on the right. Action uses semibold heading style with optional muted description subline.
slots.reagent-list: Sub-panel showing all reagents required for this step. Each ReagentRow lists name, volume, concentration, and source/lot identifier. Uses d-annotation caption 'Reagents' above the list.
slots.equipment-chips: Horizontal row of pill-shaped EquipmentChip tags with tiny icons identifying required instruments (vortex, centrifuge, pipette, heat block, incubator). Clickable to surface instrument details.
slots.safety-badges: Horizontal row of color-coded SafetyBadges (amber=PPE, red=hazard, blue=specialized). Icon + text for each. Essential for benchtop safety compliance.
slots.timer-display: Timed-preset-only slot. Large mono-data countdown/count-up timer with start/pause/reset icon buttons. Background shifts color as time elapses. Audible chime on completion.
slots.notes-box: Optional expandable textarea capturing scientist observations, deviations, or measurements at this step. Timestamped when saved.
slots.completion-check: Bottom-right large checkbox labeled 'Mark complete'. Locks step when checked and records who completed it with timestamp. Completed steps show success-tinted background.

keyboard: Tab: cycle through reagents and equipment chips

responsive.mobile: Step card expands to full-width. ReagentList collapses into expandable accordion. EquipmentChips wrap to multiple lines. TimerDisplay grows to hero size for visibility at the bench where scientists may be gloved and distant.
responsive.tablet: Full standard layout. ReagentList and EquipmentChips display side-by-side in two columns if space permits.

transitions.completion-lock: card background tint to success + border color shift 300ms ease-out
transitions.timer-warning: background color pulse to amber 400ms ease-in-out when <30s

ambient.timer-pulse: expired timer border red-glow 800ms ease-in-out infinite
ambient.running-shimmer: timer digits subtle brightness pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite while running

composition.ProtocolStep: Surface(d-surface, border-left-accent) > [StepHeader + ReagentList + EquipmentChip[] + SafetyBadge[] + TimerDisplay? + NotesBox? + CompletionCheck]
